data.title: Analyst Lifts Computacenter PT to 4,400p and Reiterates Buy on Stronger Growth Outlook
data.description:
Analyst Charles Brennan from Jefferies maintained a Buy rating on Computacenter and increased the price target to p4,400.00 from p3,700.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Charles Brennan has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that strengthen Computacenter’s investment case. He sees the company benefiting from both rising market share and increasing demand tied to large cloud providers’ data‑center investments, which together support higher growth and profitability expectations.

In response to the stronger-than-anticipated recent trading update, he lifted his earnings forecasts for fiscal years 2026 and 2027 by roughly 7–8%. Reflecting this improved outlook and his view of Computacenter as the leading “picks and shovels” operator in its sector, he also increased his price target from 3,700p to 4,400p while reiterating his Buy recommendation.

Brennan covers the Technology sector, focusing on stocks such as Computacenter, Temenos, and Bytes Technology Group Plc. According to TipRanks, Brennan has an average return of 8.8% and a 58.90% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released yesterday, UBS also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a p3,500.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Alberto Francese Reiterates Buy on First Capital After Earnings Rebound, Portfolio Value Gains, and High Dividend Support Upside to NAV-Based Target
data.description:
Intesa Sanpaolo analyst Alberto Francese maintained a Buy rating on First Capital S.p.A. today and set a price target of €27.40.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Alberto Francese has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, primarily the strong earnings rebound and value creation in the portfolio. First Capital moved from a small loss to a solid profit, largely thanks to significant fair value gains on key holdings and higher fee income and dividends, confirming the effectiveness of its investment strategy.

In addition, the company has successfully realised gains on several positions, freeing liquidity for new investments while simultaneously enhancing shareholder returns via a high dividend yield. Based on the current net asset value, Francese sees notable upside to his target price, and when combined with the proven track record and generous remuneration policy, this underpins his positive recommendation on the stock.

According to TipRanks, Francese is an analyst with an average return of -2.1% and a 34.48% success rate.
